summary: EBS volumes. Grow is guardrailed and irreversible; gp2 → gp3 is a self-service, auto-eligible cost save.

operations.title: Grow an EBS volume
operations.description: Increase a volume's size. Grow-only — you cannot shrink an EBS volume, so this is irreversible. No downtime.

params.name: volume
params.label: Volume
params.help: Select the EBS volume to enlarge; the picker lists only estate-managed volumes from inventory, and the size field separately enforces grow-only since EBS volumes can never be shrunk.

params.name: new_size_gib
params.label: New size (GiB)
params.help: Must exceed the current size; a volume cannot be shrunk.

operations.title: Migrate a volume gp2 → gp3
operations.description: Switch a gp2 volume to gp3 (cheaper, same or better baseline). Reversible, no downtime.

params.name: volume
params.label: Volume (gp2)
params.help: Select the gp2 volume to migrate; the inventory picker restricts the choice to EBS volumes already managed in the estate.

params.name: target_type
params.label: Target type
params.help: The EBS volume type to convert to, fixed by the allowlist to gp3 because this form is exactly the gp2-to-gp3 cost migration; any other type change must go through the change-volume-type operation instead.

operations.title: Delete an EBS volume
operations.description: Destroy an EBS volume. Irreversible — data is lost, so detach it first. High blast radius; an engineer authors and reviews the change.

params.name: volume
params.label: Volume
params.help: Select the EBS volume to destroy; the inventory picker limits deletion to estate-managed volumes, and because data loss is irreversible an engineer still authors and reviews the change.

operations.title: Change EBS volume type
operations.description: Change the volume type (standard/gp2/gp3/io1/io2/sc1/st1) via online ModifyVolume. Stateful config change, no downtime; AWS enforces a ~6h cooldown between modifications and the target type must be compatible with any provisioned iops/throughput. Reversible.

params.name: volume
params.label: Volume
params.help: Select the EBS volume whose type changes; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed volumes, and AWS applies it online via ModifyVolume with no downtime.

params.name: type
params.label: Volume type
params.help: gp3 supports independent iops/throughput; io1/io2 are provisioned-IOPS; sc1/st1 are throughput HDD.

operations.title: Set provisioned IOPS
operations.description: Set provisioned iops for io1/io2/gp3 volumes via online ModifyVolume. Must respect the type's IOPS:GiB ratio (gp3 ≤16000; io1/io2 up to 64000). No downtime, reversible; affects cost.

params.name: volume
params.label: Volume
params.help: Select the EBS volume to provision IOPS on; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed volumes, and the volume must be of type io1, io2, or gp3 for iops to apply.

params.name: iops
params.label: Provisioned IOPS
params.help: Only valid for type io1, io2 or gp3. Value must satisfy the type's IOPS:GiB ratio.

operations.title: Set throughput (gp3)
operations.description: Set throughput in MiB/s for gp3 volumes via online ModifyVolume. No downtime, reversible; affects cost.

params.name: volume
params.label: Volume
params.help: Select the gp3 volume whose throughput changes; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed volumes, and AWS applies it online with no downtime.

params.name: throughput
params.label: Throughput (MiB/s)
params.help: Only valid for type gp3.

operations.title: Toggle final snapshot on delete
operations.description: Enable/disable final_snapshot — whether a snapshot is captured when the volume is destroyed. Affects delete-time behavior only; no change to the live volume. Reversible, no downtime.

params.name: volume
params.label: Volume
params.help: Select the volume whose delete-time snapshot setting changes; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed volumes, and the live volume itself is not modified.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Final snapshot
params.help: Sets final_snapshot: true makes a snapshot get captured automatically if the volume is ever destroyed, preserving a last-chance recovery copy, while false skips it; a strict true/false toggle that changes delete-time behavior only.

operations.title: Add a tag
operations.description: Add a key/value tag to the volume. Cosmetic metadata; reversible, no downtime.

params.name: volume
params.label: Volume
params.help: Select the EBS volume to tag; the inventory picker keeps this metadata-only change on estate-managed volumes.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: 1-128 chars; letters, numbers, spaces and _ . : / = + @ -.

params.name: value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: Up to 256 chars.

operations.title: Remove a tag
operations.description: Remove a tag by key from the volume. Reversible, no downtime.

params.name: volume
params.label: Volume
params.help: Select the EBS volume to remove the tag from; the inventory picker keeps the change on estate-managed volumes, and a removed tag can always be re-added.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key to remove
params.help: Exact key of the tag to remove.

operations.title: Request another change to EBS volume
operations.description: Catch-all for any aws_ebs_volume change not offered as a bounded form above — including ForceNew/replacement fields (encrypted, kms_key_id, availability_zone, snapshot_id, outpost_arn, multi_attach_enabled, volume_initialization_rate) and anything else. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Set EBS volume encryption
operations.description: Set the `encrypted` argument (true/false) on an existing EBS volume. Encryption is a creation-time property on aws_ebs_volume: changing it forces Terraform to destroy and recreate the volume (ForceNew), losing existing data unless the volume is snapshotted first. High blast radius — an engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: volume
params.label: Volume
params.help: The volume to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: encrypted
params.label: Encrypted
params.help: true encrypts the disk. Changing this forces volume replacement (ForceNew); snapshot first to preserve data.

operations.title: Set EBS Multi-Attach
operations.description: Set the `multi_attach_enabled` argument (true/false) to enable or disable Amazon EBS Multi-Attach. Supported on io1 and io2 volumes only. This is a creation-time property: changing it forces Terraform to destroy and recreate the volume (ForceNew) — data is lost unless snapshotted first — and it changes concurrent-attach semantics with data-corruption potential. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: volume
params.label: Volume
params.help: The volume to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: multi_attach_enabled
params.label: Multi-Attach enabled
params.help: Enable EBS Multi-Attach (io1/io2 volumes only). Changing this forces volume replacement (ForceNew); snapshot first to preserve data.
